meta.title: meter

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 3 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: meter
root.mentor_note: meter turns up inside almost every instrument built to measure something exactly.

attempt.direction_prompt: A rough guess, or an exact measurement?

context_sentences: The pilot glanced at the altimeter and began the descent exactly on schedule.
Cloud cover made the horizon useless, so the climbers trusted only the altimeter strapped to a wrist.
By lunchtime the pedometer clipped to her belt already read well past six thousand steps.
He never quite trusted the pedometer on his phone, convinced it undercounted every uphill mile.
The electrician touched the voltmeter’s probes to the wire before letting anyone near it.
A reading of zero on the voltmeter finally confirmed that the circuit really was dead.
She switched from engineering to meteorology after one lecture on how storms actually form.
Decades of meteorology still cannot predict, more than a week out, exactly where a storm will land.

construct_options.text: The scientific study of the atmosphere and weather
The measurement of distance between falling stars
The precise measurement of time using instruments

context_sentences: The ship’s captain trusted the chronometer more than any other instrument aboard.
A single faulty chronometer was enough to send the entire survey team off course.

construct_options.text: An instrument that measures time with great precision
A calendar that marks only historic dates
A device that records a person’s heart rate
